comments.body:
I reviewed the built HCLLM paper example and captured the reusable paper-hosting workflow in PR #7.

Key takeaways captured:

- `/` should be the imported paper note itself, with normal content-page chrome instead of a special landing layout.
- The full hosted paper section tree should live under `content/<paper-slug>/`, with the folder index labeled `Sections`.
- `content/papers/` should remain the cited/reference-paper note area, not the hosted full text.
- Imported LaTeX labels like `sec:...`, `subsec:...`, `subsub:...`, and `fig:...` should be rewritten into descriptive internal links.
- Boilerplate parent/previous/next Markdown nav links should be avoided because they pollute backlinks.
- Frontmatter `title:` should be the single source of truth; no duplicate leading `#` titles.
- Graph tags should distinguish actual page types (`paper`, `paper-section`, and only `note` when synthesis notes exist).
- The root page needs the same card width/top spacing/header/sidebar/graph behavior as regular section pages.

Opened PR #7 to add the reusable skill under `.skills/literature-paper-import/`: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/7

title: Also let's add the skills for creating site from a review like paper
